name: Radar Scan
summary: A framework-neutral Cesium radar scan effect rendered with a circular ground primitive and GeoRadarScanMaterial shader.
useCases: AI analysis or prediction progress scan on a map
Area monitoring or search radius visualization
Highlighted center point with animated sweep

color.description: CSS color used by the scan shader.

type.description: Radar visual style. classic keeps the original rotating sweep, sector renders a wider scanning fan, pulse emphasizes a high-energy scan head, and grid adds a targeting grid sweep.

scanDurationMs.description: Milliseconds for one full sweep rotation.

showCenter.description: Show a small center point entity.

minimal.code:
import { createRadarScanEffect } from '@ztgkzhaohao/geo-effect-kit'

const radar = createRadarScanEffect(viewer, {
  center: { longitude: 116.391, latitude: 39.907 },
  radiusMeters: 22000,
  type: 'classic',
  color: '#36d6ff',
  scanDurationMs: 3600,
})

radar.flyTo()

// Later, when leaving the page or replacing the layer:
radar.destroy()

update.code:
radar.update({
  type: 'pulse',
  color: '#ff4fe2',
  radiusMeters: 36000,
  scanDurationMs: 1800,
  showCenter: true,
})

notes: Pass an existing Cesium Viewer; the SDK does not create or own the viewer.
Call destroy() during page cleanup to remove the primitive, data source, and render loop.
Coordinates are WGS84 longitude/latitude and are converted with Cartesian3.fromDegrees.
Use type to choose classic, sector, pulse, or grid radar styling.
Use scanDurationMs to tune sweep speed; lower values rotate faster.
